user.title: Entity Pair
user.description: Choose the two entities whose distance you want to track. Supported types: person, device tracker, sensor, or zone.

data_description.entity_a: The first entity to track. Can be a person, device tracker, sensor, or zone.
data_description.entity_b: The second entity to track. Must be different from the first.

thresholds.title: Proximity Settings
thresholds.description: Set how close the entities need to be to count as 'nearby', how far apart before they're no longer nearby, and how long to wait between updates.

data_description.entry_threshold_m: How close (in meters) the entities must be to trigger a 'nearby' event. Example: 200 m is roughly 2 city blocks.
data_description.exit_threshold_m: How far apart the entities must move before they're no longer considered nearby. Set this higher than the nearby distance to avoid rapid on/off switching. Example: 500 m means they need to move 300 m further than the entry point.
data_description.debounce_s: How many seconds to wait before acting on a new location update. This smooths out GPS jitter — phones often send several noisy updates in a row. 10 s works well for most phones.
data_description.show_advanced: Turn on to configure GPS accuracy, speed, and reliability filters on the next screen. Leave off to use sensible defaults.

advanced.title: Advanced Filters
advanced.description: These filters help ignore bad GPS data. The defaults work well for most users — only change these if you're seeing false triggers.

data.require_reliable: Only trigger when data is reliable

data_description.max_accuracy_m: Ignore location updates where the GPS error is larger than this. Example: set to 100 m to skip any fix where the device could be anywhere within a 100 m radius. A phone showing '150 m accuracy' would be skipped. Set to 0 to turn this off.
data_description.max_speed_kmh: Ignore updates that imply the device moved faster than this since the last fix. Useful to filter out GPS jumps (e.g. a phone suddenly appearing 50 km away). 1000 km/h allows flights but blocks impossible teleports. Set to 0 to turn this off.
data_description.require_reliable: When on, 'nearby' events only fire once the location data has been consistent for several updates in a row. Reduces false positives caused by a single bad GPS fix. Note: if updates are infrequent (e.g. every 3 min), this can add several minutes of delay.
data_description.min_updates_reliable: How many updates in a row are needed before the location is considered reliable. Only applies when 'Only trigger when data is reliable' is on. Example: 3 updates at 3-minute intervals = up to 9 minutes before a nearby event can fire.

error.same_entity: The two entities must be different.
error.exit_below_entry: Away distance must be greater than or equal to nearby distance.

abort.already_configured: This entity pair is already configured.
abort.already_in_progress: Setup is already in progress. Close and reopen the dialog to start over.
